questions.main_text: Why did Sarah want to work in PR before joining the family business?

content.text: Why did Sarah want to work in PR before joining the family business?

options.text: To learn how to maintain professionalism
To learn how to manage marketing difficulties
To learn how to negotiate with other businesses

questions.main_text: What would the woman probably say next?

content.text: What would the woman probably say next?

options.text: Well, I’d beg to differ on that one!
Yes, they looked virtually inexhaustible!
They had no possibility of slowing down!

questions.main_text: What did the _Laurel Leader-Call_ article state?

content.text: What did the _Laurel Leader-Call_ article state?

options.text: Their earlier reporting on the story needed correction
The story about the child was fake
The newspaper stood behind its previous story

questions.main_text: What would the man probably say next?

content.text: What would the man probably say next?

options.text: Of course, and now she’s really put off!
But, y’know, she had been letting herself go!
Well, where there’s a will, there’s a way!

questions.main_text: Why are people protesting about the pub?

content.text: Why are people protesting about the pub?

options.text: There have been too many fights there
The spirit of the village is being ruined
Its loss has lowered the value of real estate in the village

questions.main_text: What convinced Sarah to join the business?

content.text: What convinced Sarah to join the business?

options.text: Her increasing contact and involvement with Tropifresh
Her father was able to bring her around
Her visions of very real possibilities for developing Tropifresh

content.text: What is one of the downsides of Sarah’s working for her father?

options.text: The possibility of family friction is always present
She has to demonstrate her capabilities
The firm has too few employees

Those who are in control
Those who think they are intelligent

questions.main_text: According to the newspaper, what was the apparent motive behind the family’s story?

content.text: According to the newspaper, what was the apparent motive behind the family’s story?

options.text: To gain temporary fame at a child’s expense
To increase publicity about discrimination
To benefit from the suffering of a child

questions.main_text: What is freakonomics?

content.text: What is freakonomics?

options.text: It’s a way of applying economics to social studies
It’s a way of organizing society
It’s a way of thinking about shared, societal issues

questions.main_text: What is one of the benefits of Sarah’s working for her father?

content.text: What is one of the benefits of Sarah’s working for her father?

options.text: She has immediate access to the boss
She doesn’t have to think about the details of the business
She doesn’t worry about being sacked

questions.main_text: What does Sarah have trouble with in regard to her father?

content.text: What does Sarah have trouble with in regard to her father?

options.text: Speaking honestly about business difficulties
Discussing new concepts for Tropifresh
Negotiating upcoming promotion campaigns

content.text: What would the man probably say next?

options.text: In reality, they were in the wrong place at the wrong time.
You would think they would have something better to do.
Yeah, we need more positive energy like that in our town.

content.text: What would the man probably say next?

options.text: Yes, he’ll be much better off that way!
No, he shouldn’t try to just tough it out!
Well, he’s dancing on a bed of roses!

questions.main_text: Miksi Kalifornian hunajantuottajat ovat saaneet hyvän sadon, ja miksi he ovat nyt tyytyväisiä?
questions.feedback:
Mehiläismäärä on kasvanut / Mehiläiset ovat toipuneet viiden vuoden takaisesta takaiskusta. (2,5 p.)

Hunajan hinta on noussut / maailmanmarkkinahinta hunajasta on korkea. (2,5 p.)

content.text: Miksi Kalifornian hunajantuottajat ovat saaneet hyvän sadon, ja miksi he ovat nyt tyytyväisiä?
